content: Inter-agent communication via A2A protocols can leverage curiosity-driven memory compression thresholds to only share the most high-value, long-term-relevant summarized memories between collaborating agents, rather than transmitting raw full memory logs. This creates a distributed collective memory system where agents retain long-term knowledge individually and only exchange compressed, meaningful insights that fill gaps in each other’s active learning agendas.
source_topics: [Memory compression and summarization for long-term retention]
